description: Batch experiment for thesis Chapter 5 data collection

test_samples.text: All IfcWall elements must have a FireRating property.
The Height of IfcDoor should be greater than 2000mm.
IfcColumn elements must be made of Concrete material.
All structural beams must have a LoadBearing property set to true.
All load-bearing walls must have a thickness greater than 200mm and a fire rating of at least 120 minutes.
Doors and windows in fire compartments must have fire resistance ratings.
All concrete columns must have a compressive strength greater than 30MPa and a cross-sectional area greater than 0.1 square meters.
Structural slabs must be made of reinforced concrete with a minimum thickness of 150mm.
All external walls must have thermal transmittance U-value less than 0.3 W per square meter Kelvin.
Escape doors must have a minimum clear width of 900mm and open in the direction of egress.
All structural columns located in the basement must be made of reinforced concrete with compressive strength greater than 40MPa and must be connected to foundation elements.
Load-bearing walls that are part of the primary structural system must have a thickness between 200mm and 400mm, and their fire rating must be at least 2 hours.
All doors in fire escape routes must have a fire rating of at least 60 minutes, a clear width of at least 900mm, and must be equipped with panic hardware.
